data.narrative:
Antora Energy is a Series B climate and energy company that has raised $150M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of six same-stage peers in the Climate & Energy sector, including Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), alongside one public comparable (LNZA). The peer range spans $120M to $5.0B, reflecting substantial heterogeneity in market valuations for frontier-energy technologies at this stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression, using total capital raised as a valuation proxy, implies a 1.9x step-up multiplier from Series B to the next funding round, yielding $285M. Peer-median analysis across the six same-sector, same-stage comparables produced a median valuation of $355M. These methods yield a triangulated range of $242M (low), $320M (median), and $408M (high). The 69% spread between low and high estimates reflects uncertainty inherent in early-stage frontier-tech valuations, where comparable companies span an order of magnitude and post-investment performance remains unproven.

The median valuation of $320M is supported by both methods and represents the central estimate. Key sensitivities include: peer selection volatility (the $5.0B outlier significantly impacts median), near-term financing market conditions for climate tech, and technology de-risking progress specific to Antora's thermal energy storage architecture.
